sound_type: complex hour-long spiral song -- hierarchical: units, phrases, themes, songs. Cultural transmission across ocean basins. 20 Hz to 10 kHz. The most complex animal vocalization on Earth. E=8 SPS TIES ENGINE MAXIMUM
ecosystem: Open ocean. Summer feeding in PNW/Alaska. Breeding in Hawaii/Mexico. Migratory.
season: Summer (May-November) in PNW waters. Breeding/singing in winter on tropical grounds.

coordinates.description: Humpback Whale in San Juan Islands / Strait of Juan de Fuca. Summer feeding grounds. Migratory.

taxonomy_note.description: Second Balaenopteridae. E=8 SPS TIES ENGINE MAXIMUM. The most complex animal vocalization on Earth. Hierarchical song structure. Cultural transmission. 12 consecutive marine mammals (180-191).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Megaptera from Greek 'mega' (great) + 'pteron' (wing/fin) -- referring to the long pectoral fins. novaeangliae from Latin 'Nova Anglia' (New England), where the species was first scientifically described.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Megaptera -- monotypic genus. Only one species: M. novaeangliae. Recently reclassified within Balaenoptera by some authorities.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Cetacea. Balaenopteridae continues at 191. Second rorqual.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Sixth marine mammal family continues: 12 consecutive entries. E=1 to E=8 within the streak.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Marine mammal era: 12 consecutive (180-191). Full energy range E=1 to E=8.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 20-10,000 Hz. Enormous range. Infrasonic to well audible.
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Moans: 20-500 Hz. Cries: 500-4,000 Hz. Chirps: 1,000-10,000 Hz. Full range: 20 Hz to 10 kHz.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Units: 0.5-5 sec. Phrases: 5-30 sec. Themes: 1-5 min. Songs: 10-30 min. Sessions: hours to days.
song_metrics.note_structure: Hierarchical: units (individual sounds) -> phrases (repeated unit sequences) -> themes (phrase sequences) -> songs (complete performances).
song_metrics.note_types: Moans (long, low), cries (rising/falling), chirps (short, high), grunts (percussive), snores (raspy), hybrid forms.
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Predictable sequences of units, repeated 2-10+ times per phrase. 4-7 themes per song. Fixed theme order.
song_metrics.timbre: Extraordinarily varied: low moans, high cries, percussive grunts, melodic chirps. Timbral range unmatched in the animal kingdom.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=8. SPS TIES ENGINE MAXIMUM. The most complex, longest, and most culturally significant animal vocalization on Earth.
song_metrics.tempo: Variable within and across themes. Some themes rhythmic and repetitive, others flowing and melodic.
song_metrics.context: Open ocean. Males sing on breeding grounds (Hawaii/Mexico). Fragments on feeding grounds. Song = mate attraction and/or male competition.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Extensive. Populations share songs. Songs evolve over time. Trans-oceanic cultural transmission documented.
song_metrics.key_property: COMPLEX HOUR-LONG SPIRAL SONG. E=8 SPS TIES ENGINE MAXIMUM. Hierarchical: units -> phrases -> themes -> songs. Cultural transmission. 20 Hz to 10 kHz. The most complex animal vocalization. v1.49.332.

pelage.overall: Dark gray to black dorsally, white ventrally. Extremely long pectoral flippers (up to 5 m, ~1/3 body length). Knobby head with tubercles. Broad tail flukes with distinctive black-and-white pattern unique to each individual.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Long pectoral flippers. Knobby head. Broad tail flukes (unique ventral pattern). Bushy blow. Often shows flukes when diving.
pelage.swimming_pattern: Acrobatic for a large whale. Breaching (full body out of water), pectoral fin slapping, lobtailing (tail slapping), spy-hopping. Dramatic surface behaviors.

physical_description.overall: Length: 12-16 m. Weight: 25,000-40,000 kg. Large rorqual. Distinctive long pectoral flippers. Knobby head with tubercles. Broad tail flukes. 270-400 baleen plates per side. 12-36 ventral pleats.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Long pectoral fins. Knobby head. Unique fluke pattern. Bushy blow. Acrobatic breaching.
physical_description.field_id: Large dark whale with very long pectoral fins and knobby head. Shows flukes when diving (unique ventral pattern for ID). Bushy blow. Often breaches spectacularly. Summer visitor to PNW.

nodes.title: E=8 -- SPS Ties Engine Maximum
nodes.description: First E=8 on SPS axis. Ties DoNormaal (S36-188). The ceiling is now shared between engines. The most complex animal vocalization justifies the maximum.

nodes.title: Hierarchical Song Structure
nodes.description: Units -> phrases -> themes -> songs. Recursive hierarchy. 10-30 min per song. Repeated for hours. 4-7 themes. Mirrors human musical composition.

nodes.title: Cultural Transmission at Oceanic Scale
nodes.description: Songs evolve and spread across ocean basins. Cultural revolutions: entire songs replaced within weeks. Learned, not innate. The largest-scale cultural phenomenon in the animal kingdom.

nodes.title: 12 Consecutive Marine Mammals
nodes.description: Degrees 180-191. 6 families. E=1 to E=8 within the streak. Full engine energy range represented.

cross_references.relationship: BALAENOPTERIDAE PAIR! Minke (E=4, mechanical thump + ratchet) to Humpback (E=8, complex song). Same family, vast acoustic difference.
ENERGY MATCH! Both E=8. DoNormaal broke ceiling on S36. Humpback ties on SPS. Maximum now shared between engines.
E=1 TO E=8! Harbor Porpoise (E=1, silence) to Humpback (E=8, complex song). Full range within the marine mammal era. 4 degrees apart.
THE SONG PAIR! Power pop craft + whale song complexity. Song as art. E=5 + E=8 = 13 (ties record).
